headline: MediaTek hires former TSMC executive to boost AI chip packaging
summary: Taiwanese chip designer MediaTek said it has appointed former ‌TSMC executive Douglas Yu as a part-time ‌adviser as it steps up advanced packaging work and expands ​into the AI chip market.  • Yu joined TSMC in 1994 and retired in 2025, holding a range of roles in backend ‌research and development.  ⁠He played a key part in developing TSMC's advanced packaging technologies, including its ⁠CoWoS (Chip on Wafer on Substrate).

content.title: MediaTek hires former TSMC executive to boost AI chip packaging
content.summary: Taiwanese chip designer MediaTek said it has appointed former ‌TSMC executive Douglas Yu as a part-time ‌adviser as it steps up advanced packaging work and expands ​into the AI chip market.  • Yu joined TSMC in 1994 and retired in 2025, holding a range of roles in backend ‌research and development.  ⁠He played a key part in developing TSMC's advanced packaging technologies, including its ⁠CoWoS (Chip on Wafer on Substrate).

headline: This High-Yield Bond ETF Offers Steady Cash Flow and Low Volatility
summary: Income investors face a familiar bind in 2026: investment-grade bonds yield around the 10-year Treasury’s roughly 4.4%, while equities like the S&P 500 have returned roughly 28% over the past year with stomach-churning volatility along the way. headline: AI chipmaker Cerebras targets $115-$125 share price in US IPO, source says
summary: AI chipmaker Cerebras Systems, a rival to Nvidia , is scheduled to ‌kick off its IPO roadshow on Monday, ‌with shares expected to be priced between $115 and $125, a source ​familiar with the matter told Reuters.  This marks Cerebras' second attempt to go public after withdrawing a previous IPO filing last October.  Cerebras did not immediately respond ‌to Reuters request ⁠for a comment.

content.title: AI chipmaker Cerebras targets $115-$125 share price in US IPO, source says
content.summary: AI chipmaker Cerebras Systems, a rival to Nvidia , is scheduled to ‌kick off its IPO roadshow on Monday, ‌with shares expected to be priced between $115 and $125, a source ​familiar with the matter told Reuters.  This marks Cerebras' second attempt to go public after withdrawing a previous IPO filing last October.  Cerebras did not immediately respond ‌to Reuters request ⁠for a comment.
